scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of 3 years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Defendant approached the Plaintiff seeking financial assistance. The Plaintiff, carrying on occupation as {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, agreed to advance a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant, who is carrying on occupation as {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, as a hand loan.

6. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff transferred the said loan amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant via NEFT bank transfer. The transaction bears UTR Number AXIB20240315123456. The proof of the said bank transfer is annexed herewith as Annexure A.

7. Though there was no written agreement executed between the parties, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount and the liability to repay the same via WhatsApp messages. The copies of the digital communications acknowledging the debt are annexed herewith as Annexure B.

BREACH AND DEFAULT

8. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite the liability being due. The date of default for the purpose of interest calculation is stated to be 20.06.2024.

9. Repeated oral demands were made upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding loan amount. The Defendant failed to honour the commitment to repay despite such demands.

10. Subsequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant demanding repayment of the dues. The Defendant has failed to comply with the notice and has not made any repayment towards the principal amount. The copy of the legal notice is annexed herewith as Annexure C.

CLAIM FOR INTEREST

11. Due to the failure of the Defendant to repay the loan, the Plaintiff has suffered financial loss. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, till the date of realisation.

12. The Defendant is liable to pay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with the accrued interest and costs of the suit. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the said amounts from the Defendant.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The Defendant borrowed / received a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- from the Plaintiff and is liable to repay the same with interest under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

14. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

15. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

16. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

17. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Appropriate court fee has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

19. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just from the date of filing of this suit till the date of judgment / decree, under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (b) Future
